title: IBM Unveils Sub-1 Nanometer Chip Technology
kicker: Engineering breakthrough | Semiconductor innovation | Future of computing
hook: IBM's new sub-1 nm chip packs nearly 100 billion transistors, doubling density over its 2 nm predecessor and promising up to 50% more performance or 70% better energy efficiency.

beats.heading: IBM's Sub-1 Nanometer Chip: A Leap Beyond Atomic Dimensions

blocks.html: IBM has introduced a sub-1 nanometer chip that fits nearly 100 billion transistors onto a fingernail-sized die, roughly twice the density of its 2 nm chip from 2021.

blocks.text: Enabled by a series of structural and material innovations, including IBM’s groundbreaking three-dimensional nanostack architecture, the technology demonstrates how continued gains in performance and efficiency remain possible even as chip features approach atomic dimensions.

beats.heading: Nanostack Architecture: The Industry’s First 3D Nanosheet Design

blocks.html: Researchers developed a new transistor architecture called nanostack, the industry’s first known three-dimensional, nanosheet-based design that vertically stacks and staggers transistors for higher density.

blocks.text: The nanostack design unlocks different material combinations within each stacked layer, optimizing performance and power efficiency independently.

blocks.html: The nanostack architecture was experimentally validated through ultra-thin dielectric bonding, dual-channel engineering capability, and functional CMOS inverter operation with expected switching performance.

items.title: IBM debuts sub-1 nanometer chip technology

blocks.html: In research presented at VLSI 2026, IBM showed a 40% scaling in SRAM with the nanostack architecture, enabling more efficient chips for advanced AI workloads.

blocks.text: With this breakthrough structure, logic technology can extend below the 1 nm node, advancing angstrom-level scaling and projecting at least a decade of future scaling.

beats.heading: Partnerships and Facilities Driving Sub-1 nm Production

blocks.html: IBM partners with Lam Research, Tokyo Electron, and SCREEN Semiconductor Solutions to develop new High NA EUV processes essential for sub‑1 nm chip manufacturing.

items.title: IBM debuts sub-1 nanometer chip technology

beats.heading: Path to Production and Quantum Foundry Initiative

blocks.html: IBM anticipates early adoption of nanostack technology at the sub-1 nm node, with potential production as soon as the next 5 years.

blocks.text: Additionally, IBM is forming Anderon, a pure-play quantum foundry to manufacture most of the world’s quantum wafers, further solidifying its leadership in semiconductor R&D.

sources.title: IBM debuts sub-1 nanometer chip technology
